learning_outcomes.description: Appreciate how business plans are developed in biotechnology companies, largely in the context of the Australian biotechnology scene.
Develop the ability to operate as an independent learner aware of continual self-development.
Identify and effectively use resources, such as those within libraries, online and through consultants, to find information required.
Develop confidence to make decisions with incomplete information and where there are multiple decisions.
Develop skills to assess market and commercial opportunities for new biotechnology products.
Accurately and succinctly self-reflect and communicate to others important points you have learned.
Have fundamental knowledge of making decisions in biotechnology commercialisation through sound financial understanding.

assessments.task_description:
The assessment task "Commercialisation Plan" provides the opportunity to improve your desktop research skills, analytical skills, and complex reasoning skills. Working in groups, students will assess the market opportunity for, competitor situation of, and research and development (R&D) required (including regulatory requirements) to develop biotechnology products. Detailed instructions on how to complete this task will be provided on Blackboard.

This assignment is to be completed in groups, and your group mark is subject to moderation by a Peer Assessment Factor (PAF).

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Commercialisation Plan through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. As this is a group assignment, one group member will be responsible for submission on behalf of the entire group. It is your responsibility to confirm with your group members that submission has been successful.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The assessment item "IRR Assignment" provides the opportunity to improve your skills in financial analysis of biotechnology products and decision-making. In this assessment you will generate excel spreadsheets incorporating product development costs, calculate Internal Rates of Return (IRR), conduct sensitivity analyses, and make recommendations based on your calculations. Your answers will be submitted via a Blackboard Test. Detailed information and instructions on how to complete this task will be provided on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will be online via a Blackboard test - students will input answers obtained from excel spreadsheet analyses, submit example spreadsheets as an excel file, and input written short essay answers to questions based on the answers obtained from excel spreadsheet analyses.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The examination samples knowledge from all modules (weeks 2 - 12), the Course Introduction and Overview lecture in week 1, and Course Revision and Exam Preparation lecture in week 13. More information will be provided during teaching activities and/or on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Introduction to Commercialisation

In this module, students will be introduced to the course, assessment items and expectations (week 1) and then learn about why commercialisation is important, major commercialisation pathways for new biotechnology products emerging from both universities and research and development companies, and the biosciences value chain which underpins our learning journey (weeks 1 and 2).

Learning outcomes: L01, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Introduction to Commercialisation In this module, students will be introduced to the course, assessment items and expectations (week 1) and then learn about why commercialisation is important, major commercialisation pathways for new biotechnology products emerging from both universities and research and development companies, and the biosciences value chain which underpins our learning journey (weeks 1 and 2).

learning_activities.topic:
Workshops

In additional to lecture activities, each week we will apply our learnings and undertake activities relating to market and competitor research, identifying and planning commercialisation activities through a regulatory activity lens, budgeting, performing financial analysis, through case studies and online activities. Note there is no workshop activity in week 1 or week 13.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshops In additional to lecture activities, each week we will apply our learnings and undertake activities relating to market and competitor research, identifying and planning commercialisation activities through a regulatory activity lens, budgeting, performing financial analysis, through case studies and online activities. Note there is no workshop activity in week 1 or week 13.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: Markets & Competitors

In this module, we start with the end in mind. Students will be introduced to the importance of understanding market opportunities and competitor situations for biotechnology products once they have completed their commercialisation journeys, and the importance of gaining this understanding early.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2: Markets & Competitors In this module, we start with the end in mind. Students will be introduced to the importance of understanding market opportunities and competitor situations for biotechnology products once they have completed their commercialisation journeys, and the importance of gaining this understanding early.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: Developing and Testing Biotech Products

In this module, students will explore regulatory requirements for medical, pharmaceutical, and agricultural-based biotechnology products, and how these regulatory requirements inform commercialisation activities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3: Developing and Testing Biotech Products In this module, students will explore regulatory requirements for medical, pharmaceutical, and agricultural-based biotechnology products, and how these regulatory requirements inform commercialisation activities.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 4: Costings of R&D and Production

In this module, students will learn that scale-up and production are important elements for any commercialisation strategy. As is budgeting how much and how long the commercialisation journey will take.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 4: Costings of R&D and Production In this module, students will learn that scale-up and production are important elements for any commercialisation strategy. As is budgeting how much and how long the commercialisation journey will take.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 5: Financial analysis

In this module, we break down financial analysis and forecasting returns on investments for biotechnology products. We also explore different risk levels and how they affect financial modelling, and how this impacts overall business models at the firm level.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 5: Financial analysis In this module, we break down financial analysis and forecasting returns on investments for biotechnology products. We also explore different risk levels and how they affect financial modelling, and how this impacts overall business models at the firm level.

learning_activities.topic:
Course Revision and Exam Preparation

In this last lecture, we review content taught throughout the course, completing our journey along the biosciences value chain to understand how biotechnology products advance towards the market.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Revision and Exam Preparation In this last lecture, we review content taught throughout the course, completing our journey along the biosciences value chain to understand how biotechnology products advance towards the market.
